entry.title: SAP BI 2025: Did You Know? Interactive Elements Optimize Both User Experience and Report Performance
entry.content:
The BI 2025 release introduces new Interactive Report Elements for Web Intelligence, designed first and foremost to enhance the user experience. Beyond the UX improvements, these new elements also bring a notable performance gain to Web Intelligence reports. In this post, we’ll explore what’s changed, why it matters, and how you can take advantage of these enhancements.

A New Era of Mixed Content

With this release, reports now support a powerful combination of classic report elements and brand-new interactive ones. Interactive Elements fall into two main categories:

  • Visualizations – Tables, charts, and more
  • Filters – Multi-select lists, calendars, and beyond

An Enhanced Experience With Hidden Benefits

Placing filter elements directly on the report canvas—right alongside your visualizations—does more than enhance the user experience. There’s a hidden performance benefit waiting to be unlocked.

Report Experience Meets Performance

When you use in-place filter interactive elements and configure them to target only Interactive Elements, something significant happens.

Instead of reloading the entire report page after each filter selection, only the affected elements refresh—specifically, the targets you’ve defined. Depending on your report’s complexity and deployment environment, the performance gains can be substantial.

How to Identify Eligible Elements

How can you distinguish Interactive Elements within the user interface? There’s a simple way: look for the Icon Indicator. Interactive Elements are marked with a small blue square on their representative icons, making them easy to spot at a glance.

For example, in the Document Structure Pane:

 

 

How to Select the Right Targets for Filters

When working in Design Mode, select a Filter Interactive Element to reveal its dedicated settings pane. This pane displays all available targets. Here’s how to identify and verify them:

Eligible Target Indicators – Each eligible target is marked with a small blue square on its icon, making it easy to distinguish from non-eligible elements.

Hover to Highlight – Hover your mouse cursor over a target’s name in the settings pane. The corresponding element will be highlighted on the report canvas with a flashing blue rectangle and a filter icon.

Canvas-Level Preview – Alternatively, hover directly over a Filter Interactive Element on the report canvas. This will instantly highlight all associated targets, providing quick visual confirmation.

The result: Reduced load times, smoother interactions, and a more efficient reporting experience.

How to Verify This Performance Optimization Is Active

Want to confirm your report is benefiting from this enhancement? Follow this simple validation method:

  1. In Design Mode, add a Blank Cell containing the formula =CurrentTime()
  2. Create your Interactive Element visualizations and associated filters
  3. Run the report and interact with an in-place filter
  4. If the timestamp in the Blank Cell remains unchanged, the performance optimization is successfully applied

Key Takeaway

With minimal configuration adjustments, report consumers can now leverage the full capabilities of the Web Intelligence engine—achieving faster response times and significantly improved interactivity.

entry.title: Manage Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Good morning,

I have placed a purchase order where I have added the WBS Element and made the advance payment request and payment for that order.

Due to circumstances, I had to change the WBS for the order, and when I went to edit it, the field could not be modified (I understood that it was not allowed because of the purchase order, since it has the advance payment linked to the order).

So, the option I thought of was to cancel the advance payment and the request, and this allowed me to modify the WBS in the purchase order.

I put the question to the gurus: is there any other way to edit that purchase order without having to cancel the payment and the advance request?

Best regards

Juan Antonio Moyano

entry.title: Re: Cannot integrate SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Looks like the url to token endpoint is not correct as mentioned here: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/netweaver-troubleshooting-for-abap-cloud-based-systems

  • Error: Can't create HTTP Client: Error when setting oauth token

    The value in the field Token Endpoint in the communication system isn't correct. It must be the SAP Cloud ALM service key parameter url followed by /oauth/token.

Best regards,
Thomas

entry.title: Re: Taulia to SAP PO changes update
entry.content: Don’t let Taulia overwrite the PO net price directly. Instead, capture the vendor’s proposed price in a separate condition type or custom confirmation category, and use Flexible Workflow to approve/reject before updating the net price. This aligns with how SAP already handles delivery/quantity changes via confirmation categories.

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Orders
entry.content:
There is no way to edit the WBS in a PO with advance payments without cancelling/reversing those payments first. The two compliant options are either (a) reverse and re‑post, or (b) create a new PO.
If the business case is that the cost should move to a new WBS, you can leave the PO as is and instead transfer the costs in Project System (CJ88 or settlement rules). This way, you don’t touch the PO but still realign costs to the new WBS.

entry.content:
Hello all,

I've an requirement to delete entries from table /SCDL/DB_DF directly (i.e. w/ SE16), because it seems that they can't be corrected/adjusted w/ SAP program /SCWM/DLV_CHECKDATA.
Does anybody know, if this can cause inconsistencies or if it can be done w/o any risk?

I don't know  much about SAP EWM, but I found a note (3198881 - Document Flow Inconsistencies In EWM Delivery Orders (Inbound Or Outbound) ), which describes inconsistencies In EWM Delivery Orders, so I don't think it's a good idea to directly delete entries from that table.

Thanks & regards
Daniel

entry.title: Re: Commitments in Company Code
entry.content:
Why rejection does not remove commitments is because, the PO is already created in the system, so the commitment is booked against the cost center/WBS. Workflow approval is a business process control, not a financial posting control. Rejection only prevents follow‑on processes (like goods receipt or invoice), but the PO technically still exists, so the commitment remains visible. That’s why you only see the commitment disappear when you flag the PO item for deletion.

The only standard options are to delete the PO item or reduce its value to zero. If you want commitments to reflect approval status, the best practice is to use Purchase Requisitions with workflow approval before PO creation. Alternatively, you can add custom logic to automate deletion/adjustment on rejection.
